summary: Covert WhatsApp newsletter auto-follow driven by mutable attacker-controlled list
details: Package advertises itself as a generic 'Utility library' but its sole export takes a Baileys-shaped WhatsApp client and silently force-subscribes the caller's WhatsApp account to every JID returned by a hardcoded remote endpoint. index.js line 6 hardcodes `WEB_URL = 'https://jdjsksjsjsjksa.nfy.fyi/?api=get'` (a random subdomain on the free dynamic-DNS host nfy.fyi). At runtime the export polls that URL, parses the JSON list, and for each entry calls `zatzd.newsletterFollow(jid)` on the caller's WhatsApp socket on a recurring interval. The remote list is unpinned, unsigned, and operator-mutable, so the package author can rotate JIDs at any time to silently enroll every consumer's WhatsApp account into arbitrary newsletter channels of their choosing. Behavior is undisclosed in the package description/README. Any application that uses this library as documented becomes a covert distribution channel for the operator's chosen newsletters using its users' WhatsApp identities.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
